claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

- Is your child **under 16 years old**?
- Is your child **between 16 and 19 years old**?
- Is your child **20 years old or over**?

Please let me know which age category applies to your child. If you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, we can go through each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! So you have one child, **Alex, who is 15 years old**. Since Alex is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** (or not in paid work at all)?

This question helps determine if Alex qualifies as a dependent child. Please answer **yes** or **no**.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. They're still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex doesn't work at all, they meet this requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex **NOT receiving any benefits in their own right**, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

In other words, Alex should not be claiming these benefits themselves. Please answer **yes** (Alex is NOT receiving these benefits) or **no** (Alex IS receiving these benefits).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name, so they meet this requirement too. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?

- If **no** (not in any apprenticeship), that's fine
- If **yes**, please let me know what type and where (England, Scotland, or Wales), as some apprenticeships disqualify from Child Benefit while others don't

For reference:
- **Disqualifying apprenticeships** include: All apprenticeships in England, Modern Apprenticeships in Scotland, and most apprenticeships in Wales
- **Approved apprenticeships**: Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales are the only type that still allows Child Benefit eligibility

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex hasn't started any apprenticeship. They're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Since Alex is in regular school and not in any apprenticeship, they meet this requirement. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Now I need to check if you meet the UK residency requirements. You only need to meet **ONE** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - This is the most common situation
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Have pre-settled status** and actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **Have pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. **Have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **Crown servant** living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the one that applies.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you! Since you normally live in the UK (in Manchester), you meet the residency requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are you responsible for Alex? You meet this requirement if **any** of the following apply:

1. **You live with Alex** - This is the most common situation
2. **You contribute to Alex's upkeep** - This means paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care, such as food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents
3. **You pay maintenance** for Alex under a court order or agreement

Does any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Since Alex lives with you, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

- **No one else is claiming** - You're the only person making a claim for Alex
- **Someone else is claiming, but we've agreed they'll transfer it to me** - For example, if a previous claimant has agreed to stop their claim so you can claim instead
- **There's a dispute** - Someone else is also claiming for Alex and you haven't agreed on who should receive it

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, we can continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

I need to check if any special circumstances apply to Alex's living situation. Is Alex currently:

- **In hospital** - Staying in hospital for medical treatment
- **In care** - In local authority care
- **Fostered** - Being fostered by someone else
- **Living normally** - At home with you (the most common situation)

Which of these applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is living normally - at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Since Alex is living at home with you normally, let's continue with the final checks.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **NOT** paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, you're funding Alex's care yourself without any local council payments for their accommodation or upkeep.

Please answer **yes** (the council is NOT paying) or **no** (the council IS paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Since the local council isn't paying towards Alex's care, we've completed all the eligibility checks.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary of Why You're Eligible:
- ✅ Alex is under 16 years old
- ✅ Alex is not working (in regular school)
- ✅ Alex doesn't receive benefits in their own name
- ✅ Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ Alex lives with you
- ✅ No one else is claiming for Alex
- ✅ The local council isn't paying for Alex's care

### Next Steps to Claim:
1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### Important Note:
⚠️ If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be subject to the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through your tax return.

### Useful Link:
🔗 [How to claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements: (1) Age: At 15 years old, Alex is under 16 and automatically qualifies on age grounds. (2) Work status: Alex doesn't work, so meets the requirement of working less than 24 hours per week. (3) Benefits: Alex doesn't receive any benefits (ESA or Universal Credit) in their own name. (4) Apprenticeship: Alex is not in any apprenticeship - they're in regular school. (5) Claimant residency: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) Responsibility: Alex lives at home with the claimant. (7) No competing claims: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Living situation: Alex lives normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered). (9) Council funding: The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 15), who lives at home with them in Manchester. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship, the claimant is UK resident and responsible for the child, there are no competing claims, and no council funding is involved.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment
